channel.title: manual on ecmccfg
channel.description: Recent content in manual on ecmccfg

item.title: build at PSI
item.description: These instructions are PSI-specific. Scope This page describes the local PSI build and test workflow for ecmccfg. Build The normal build path uses driver.makefile. Current assumptions: Debian 10 or Debian 12 EPICS >= R7.0.7 Build on the login cluster with: make [LIBVERSION] [clean] [uninstall] install Check a particular version To check that a specific module version loads, you can start an IOC shell with explicit versions.

item.title: Quickstart
item.description: Goal Bring up an IOC with a single axis using the YAML-based configuration. This page is intentionally short. For a more detailed walkthrough of the full startup structure, see introduction. For reusable public examples, see examples. Prerequisites hugo/Readme.md steps completed (repo cloned, prerequisites installed) Working EtherCAT master and reachable slaves One YAML parser backend: jinja (default): Python 3 with jinja2 (auto-installed by the axis loader) ecb: C++ backend (ECMC_CFG_TOOL=ecb, see ecb) Steps Load the module version you want: require ecmccfg <optional VERSION> Add the coupler and the relevant slaves (example: coupler + EL1018 + EL7062): ${SCRIPTEXEC} ${ecmccfg_DIR}addSlave.

item.title: introduction
item.description: This page describes the detailed IOC startup structure and the main classic entry points. If you want the shortest current path for a new configuration, start with Quickstart and Examples first. If any naming is unclear, see Terminology. Principle EtherCAT requires the field bus components (slaves) to be configured on the master. The master must know about the data exchanges with the slaves, this is referred to as process image.

item.title: best practice
item.description: Scope This page is the entry point for the public best-practice material in the manual. Use best-practice pages when you want: a recommended starting point for a new configuration example-driven guidance rather than full reference material public examples that correspond to the manual text By area General host setup, EtherCAT rate, and controller-side recommendations Motion axis, encoder, homing, motor-record, and motion example patterns PLC PLC structure, macros, debug handling, and EPICS variable exposure Core recommendations Some recurring recommendations apply across several sections:

item.title: Terminology
item.description: This page defines the main terms used across the manual. Preferred terms axis PLC An axis PLC is PLC logic attached directly to one axis as part of the motion configuration. Use it for axis-local logic such as local interlocks, setpoint conditioning, or small axis-specific sequences. best-practice example A best-practice example is a public, reusable example under examples/PSI/best_practice/. These examples should be self-contained and are the preferred examples to reference from the manual.

item.title: Examples
item.description: How to use this page Prefer examples/PSI/best_practice/ as the public, reusable starting point for new configurations. Pick the example that is closest to your hardware or use case, copy it into your IOC area, and then adapt the macros, component selection, and YAML files. Start from these first I want a minimal axis example: Quickstart I want stepper motion with common Beckhoff terminals: Stepper BiSS-C I want incremental stepper examples: EL7047 + EL5102 or EL7062 I want PLC examples: basic PLCs or master-less PLC I want data storage: data storage buffer Motion examples Stepper BiSS-C EL7041/EL5042 and EL7062/EL5042 examples Stepper incremental, EL7047 + EL5102 Stepper incremental, EL7062 SmarAct MCS2 PVT profile move, EL7041-0052 Stepper open loop + autosave restore Stepper open loop + retries Stepper without motor record Servo CSV Hardware substitution template Synchronization examples Slit synchronization Mirror synchronization PLC and general examples Basic PLC examples Master-less PLC example Motion sequence PLC example Data storage buffer Legacy ESS examples The examples/ESS/ tree is mainly useful when reading or maintaining older classic motion configurations based on .

item.title: Upgrades
item.description: Purpose Collect upgrade notes and migration checklists for major versions. Release Notes New features are described in ecmc RELEASE.md: https://github.com/epics-modules/ecmc/blob/master/RELEASE.md v10 -> v11 Highlights CSP-PC mode added (centralized position loop in CSP) Position-velocity-time (profile move) support Motor record PID fields still limited to 0..1.0; use values 100x smaller than native ecmc settings Be careful when using motor record PID fields. Example: ecmc Kp=1.0 corresponds to motor record PCOF=0.01.
